title: 5 Logging in to an Application Protected by 2-Step Security
body_text:
5	Logging in to an Application Protected by 2-Step Security
5.1	Logging in to an Application Protected by 2-Step Security
Users must have a 2-step security token assigned to their Client Centre User ID to access certain applications (e.g., User Management), or functionality within an application (e.g., approving outgoing wires in the Wires application) thatâs protected by 2-step security. A User Management Security Officer (Security Officer) assigns a 2-step security token to a user via User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures.
Log in to an application protected by 2-step security as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Entering Credentials
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select the appropriate application from the displayed list (e.g., User Management).
Enter the code thatâs generated on your registered 2-step security token, then select
Done
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Some applications (e.g., Wires) allow you to login with your password, but will prompt you to enter a 2-step security code when you access certain functionality within an application (e.g., approving outgoing wires) thatâs protected by 2-step security.
Figure 3: Entering the Code from the 2-Step Security Token
END
